page_content: TLPL 489G (https://app.testudo.umd.edu/soc/search/? courseId=TLPL489G) (Internship in Education: Elementary Education Residency) Taken in both the fall and spring of the senior year, this course is the fourth and ﬁfth of ﬁve ﬁeld-based professional experiences for students who are degree seeking in the Elementary Education Teacher Preparation Program. Residents will participate throughout the year in all aspects of a teacher's life in the school. In the fall semester, Resident Interns are beginning to practice the art and craft of teaching under the supervision of a mentor teacher. In the spring semester (student teaching) Resident Interns gradually assume full responsibility for classroom management, planning, delivery of instruction and assessment with continual guidance from a mentor teacher. Teacher candidates complete the yearlong internship in a network of professional development school settings (PDS Networks). These sites reflect the linguistic, ethnic and economic diversity that is characteristic of the Greater Baltimore-Washington D.C. region. For additional information about the yearlong internship, see the College of Education entry.
